---
title: "figure2_heatmap"
author: "Ruwen Zhou"
date: "11/8/2024"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

library(readr)
library(plyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(dplyr)
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(phyloseq)
library(vegan)
library(DESeq2)
library(pheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)
```


#demographic
```{r}
helios_core = read_excel("data/helios800.xlsx")
metadata_micro = read_csv("data/metadata_fil_01032024_redacted.csv")
sample_list = metadata_micro$`Original Sample name`
helios_new <- filter(helios_core, FREG1_Barcode %in% sample_list)
```
#food nutrition
```{r}
FFQ_food = read_xlsx("data/HELIOS_FFQA.xlsx")
FFQ_nutrition = read_xlsx("data/HELIOS_FFQB.xlsx")
pid_list = helios_new$FREG0_PID
food_new = filter(FFQ_food, FREG0_PID %in% pid_list)
nutrition_new = filter(FFQ_nutrition, FREG0_PID %in% pid_list)
```
#species
```{r}
species = read_csv("data/abundance_bracken_standard_16_20221209_taxa_G_unfiltered_reads_decont2.csv")
#species$`SAM id`
helios_new_list = helios_new$FREG1_Barcode
#sample_list = metadata_micro$`Original Sample name`
micro_list <- filter(metadata_micro, metadata_micro$`Original Sample name` %in% helios_new_list)
species_new = filter(species, species$`SAM id` %in% micro_list$`Library ID`)
species_new
```

```{r}
new_merge = merge(metadata_micro, species_new, by.x = "Library ID", by.y = "SAM id")
#new_merge
species_meta = merge(x = helios_new[,c("FREG1_Barcode", "FREG5_Ethnic_Group")], y = new_merge, by.y = "Original Sample name", by.x = "FREG1_Barcode")
species_meta = species_meta %>% drop_na(FREG5_Ethnic_Group)
species_meta
```

```{r}
metadata = species_meta[, c("FREG1_Barcode", "FREG5_Ethnic_Group")]
metadata = metadata[!duplicated(metadata$FREG1_Barcode),]
metadata

drop = c("Library ID", "SampleType", "seq_batch", "DLAB25_Gluf_Mmol_L", "FREG5_Ethnic_Group","FREG10_Fasting_Hours", "FBP16_Caffeine")
species_data = species_meta[,!(names(species_meta) %in% drop)]
species_data  = species_data [!duplicated(species_data$FREG1_Barcode),]
species_data
```

#diabetes
```{r}
# metadata$diabetes_status <- ifelse(metadata$DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent > 6.4, "diabetes",
#                                    ifelse(metadata$DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent >= 5.7 & metadata$DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent <= 6.4, "pre-diabetes",
#                                           ifelse(metadata$DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent < 5.7, "normal", NA)))
# 
# #metadata$diabetes_status <- ifelse(metadata$DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent > 6.4, "diabetes",
#                                    #ifelse(metadata$DLAB27_Hba1C_Percent <= 6.4, "non-diabetes", NA))
# 
# 
# 
# metadata$glucose <- ifelse(metadata$DLAB25_Gluf_Mmol_L > 7, "diabetes",
#                                    ifelse(metadata$DLAB25_Gluf_Mmol_L >= 5.6 & metadata$DLAB25_Gluf_Mmol_L <= 7, "pre-diabetes",
#                                           ifelse(metadata$DLAB25_Gluf_Mmol_L >= 3.9 & metadata$DLAB25_Gluf_Mmol_L <= 5.6, "normal",
#                                                  ifelse(metadata$DLAB25_Gluf_Mmol_L < 3.9, "hypoglycemia", NA))))
# 
#                                          
# 
# metadata = metadata[!duplicated(metadata$FREG1_Barcode), ]
# metadata
# species_data = species_data[!duplicated(species_data$FREG1_Barcode), ]
# species_data

```

```{r}
rownames(species_data) = species_data[,1]
species_data[1] = NULL
rownames(metadata) = metadata[,1]
species_data
metadata
```


```{r}
OTU = otu_table(as.matrix(species_data), taxa_are_rows = FALSE)              
SAM = sample_data(metadata, errorIfNULL = TRUE)

phylo <- phyloseq(OTU, SAM)
phylo
phylo = filter_taxa(phylo, function(x) sum(x > 2) > (0.11 * length(x)), TRUE)
```


## Drop species that are present in less than 5% of the samples
## Drop species with a total abundance less than 0.01% of the total reads
```{r}
prevalence_threshold <- 0.1
phyloseq_filtered <- filter_taxa(phylo, 
                                  function(x) sum(x > 0) > (prevalence_threshold * nsamples(otu_table(phylo))),
                                  prune=TRUE)
phyloseq_filtered

# abundance_threshold <- 0.0001
# phyloseq_filtered <- filter_taxa(otu_table_filtered, 
#                                   function(x) sum(x) > (abundance_threshold * sum(otu_table(phylo))),
#                                   prune=TRUE)
# 
# phyloseq_filtered
phyloseq_filtered <- subset_samples(phyloseq_filtered, FREG5_Ethnic_Group != "O")

```

```{r}
# Define SCFA-producing genera (common patterns to look for in taxa names)
scfa_patterns <- c(
    "Faecalibacterium", "Roseburia", "Eubacterium", "Coprococcus",
    "Anaerostipes", "Butyricicoccus", "Butyrivibrio", "Subdoligranulum",
    "Blautia", "Ruminococcus", "Bacteroides", "Prevotella",
    "Bifidobacterium", "Lactobacillus", "Akkermansia"
)

# Function to find differentially abundant taxa
get_differential_taxa <- function(phyloseq_obj, variable, padj_cutoff = 0.05) {
    # Convert to DESeq2 object
    dds <- phyloseq_to_deseq2(phyloseq_obj, design = as.formula(paste("~", variable)))
    
    # Estimate size factors and dispersions
    dds <- estimateSizeFactors(dds)
    dds <- estimateDispersions(dds)
    
    # Perform Wald test
    dds <- DESeq(dds)
    
    # Get results
    res <- results(dds)
    
    # Order by adjusted p-value
    res_ordered <- res[order(res$padj), ]
    
    # Filter significant taxa
    significant_taxa <- rownames(res_ordered)[which(res_ordered$padj < padj_cutoff)]
    
    return(significant_taxa)
}

# Get differentially abundant taxa
significant_taxa <- get_differential_taxa(phyloseq_filtered, "FREG5_Ethnic_Group")

# If too many significant taxa, take top 30 (leaving room for SCFA producers)
if(length(significant_taxa) > 30) {
    significant_taxa <- significant_taxa[1:30]
}

# Get all taxa names
all_taxa_names <- colnames(otu_table(phyloseq_filtered))

# Find SCFA-producing taxa by searching for patterns in taxa names
scfa_taxa <- all_taxa_names[sapply(all_taxa_names, function(x) {
    any(sapply(scfa_patterns, function(pattern) grepl(pattern, x, ignore.case = TRUE)))
})]

# Combine significant taxa and SCFA producers, removing duplicates
selected_taxa <- unique(c(significant_taxa, scfa_taxa))

# Extract abundance matrix for selected taxa
abundance_mat <- otu_table(phyloseq_filtered)
abundance_mat <- as(abundance_mat, "matrix")  # Convert to matrix if needed
abundance_mat <- abundance_mat[, selected_taxa]  # Select taxa (columns)

# Transform to relative abundance
abundance_mat <- sweep(abundance_mat, 1, rowSums(abundance_mat), '/') * 100

# Get sample metadata
sample_data <- data.frame(sample_data(phyloseq_filtered))

# Create annotation dataframe for samples
annotation_row <- data.frame(
    Ethnicity = sample_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group,
    row.names = rownames(sample_data)
)

# Create annotation for taxa (marking SCFA producers)
annotation_col <- data.frame(
    Type = ifelse(selected_taxa %in% scfa_taxa, "SCFA Producer", "Differential"),
    row.names = selected_taxa
)

# Create color schemes
annotation_colors <- list(
    Ethnicity = c("C" = "#9FDDCA", "I" = "#9EA3F1", "M" = "#E17887"),
    Type = c(`SCFA Producer` = "#FDB863", Differential = "#B2ABD2")
)
```


```{r}
# Create heatmap
pheatmap(abundance_mat,
         scale = "column",  # Changed from "col" to "column"
         annotation_row = annotation_row,
         annotation_col = annotation_col,
         cluster_rows = FALSE,  # Don't cluster samples
         cluster_cols = TRUE,   # Cluster taxa
         show_rownames = FALSE, # Hide sample names for clarity
         annotation_colors = annotation_colors,
         main = "Species Abundance Across Ethnic Groups\n(Including SCFA Producers)",
         fontsize_col = 8,
         angle_col = 45)

# Print summary of included taxa
cat("\nNumber of differentially abundant taxa:", length(significant_taxa))
cat("\nNumber of SCFA-producing taxa found:", length(intersect(selected_taxa, scfa_taxa)))
cat("\nTotal number of taxa in heatmap:", length(selected_taxa))
```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(DESeq2)
library(pheatmap)
library(tidyverse)

# Define SCFA-producing genera
scfa_patterns <- c(
    "Faecalibacterium", "Roseburia", "Eubacterium", "Coprococcus",
    "Anaerostipes", "Butyricicoccus", "Butyrivibrio", "Subdoligranulum",
    "Blautia", "Ruminococcus", "Bacteroides", "Prevotella",
    "Bifidobacterium", "Lactobacillus", "Akkermansia"
)

# Function to find differentially abundant taxa
get_differential_taxa <- function(phyloseq_obj, variable, padj_cutoff = 0.05) {
    dds <- phyloseq_to_deseq2(phyloseq_obj, design = as.formula(paste("~", variable)))
    dds <- estimateSizeFactors(dds)
    dds <- estimateDispersions(dds)
    dds <- DESeq(dds)
    res <- results(dds)
    res_ordered <- res[order(res$padj), ]
    significant_taxa <- rownames(res_ordered)[which(res_ordered$padj < padj_cutoff)]
    return(significant_taxa)
}

# Get differentially abundant taxa
significant_taxa <- get_differential_taxa(phyloseq_filtered, "FREG5_Ethnic_Group")

# If too many significant taxa, take top 30
if(length(significant_taxa) > 30) {
    significant_taxa <- significant_taxa[1:30]
}

# Get all taxa names and find SCFA producers
all_taxa_names <- colnames(otu_table(phyloseq_filtered))
# scfa_taxa <- all_taxa_names[sapply(all_taxa_names, function(x) {
#     any(sapply(scfa_patterns, function(pattern) grepl(pattern, x, ignore.case = TRUE)))
# })]
scfa_taxa = scfa_patterns
# Combine significant taxa and SCFA producers
selected_taxa <- unique(c(significant_taxa, scfa_taxa))

# Extract and process abundance matrix
abundance_mat <- otu_table(phyloseq_filtered)
abundance_mat <- as(abundance_mat, "matrix")
abundance_mat <- abundance_mat[, selected_taxa]

# Convert to relative abundance
abundance_mat <- sweep(abundance_mat, 1, rowSums(abundance_mat), '/') * 100

# Get sample metadata and order it
sample_data <- data.frame(sample_data(phyloseq_filtered))
sample_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group <- factor(sample_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group, 
                                       levels = c("C", "I", "M"))

# Order samples by ethnicity
sample_order <- rownames(sample_data)[order(sample_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group)]
abundance_mat <- abundance_mat[sample_order, ]

# Transpose the matrix
abundance_mat <- t(abundance_mat)

# Create annotation dataframe for columns (samples)
annotation_col <- data.frame(
    Ethnicity = sample_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group[match(colnames(abundance_mat), rownames(sample_data))],
    row.names = colnames(abundance_mat)
)

# Create annotation for rows (taxa)
annotation_row <- data.frame(
    Type = ifelse(rownames(abundance_mat) %in% scfa_taxa, "SCFA Producer", "Differential"),
    row.names = rownames(abundance_mat)
)

# Create color schemes
annotation_colors <- list(
    Ethnicity = c("C" = "#9FDDCA", "I" = "#9EA3F1", "M" = "#E17887"),
    Type = c(`SCFA Producer` = "#FDB863", Differential = "#B2ABD2")
)

# Create custom color palette for the heatmap
color_palette <- colorRampPalette(c("#2166AC", "white", "#B2182B"))(100)



# Save the heatmap
# pdf("figure/abundance_heatmap.pdf", width = 15, height = 10)
# pheatmap(abundance_mat,
#          scale = "row",  # Scale by row since we transposed
#          color = color_palette,
#          breaks = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 101),
#          annotation_col = annotation_col,
#          annotation_row = annotation_row,
#          cluster_cols = FALSE,  # Don't cluster samples
#          cluster_rows = TRUE,   # Cluster taxa
#          show_colnames = FALSE, # Hide sample names
#          annotation_colors = annotation_colors,
#          main = "Species Abundance Across Ethnic Groups\n(Including SCFA Producers)",
#          fontsize = 10,
#          annotation_names_row = TRUE)
# dev.off()
```


```{r}
# Also save as PNG for easy viewing
png("figure/abundance_heatmap.png", width = 2000, height = 1000, res = 150)
pheatmap(abundance_mat,
         scale = "row",  # Scale by row since we transposed
         color = color_palette,
         breaks = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 101),
         annotation_col = annotation_col,
         annotation_row = annotation_row,
         cluster_cols = FALSE,  # Don't cluster samples
         cluster_rows = TRUE,   # Cluster taxa
         show_colnames = FALSE, # Hide sample names
         annotation_colors = annotation_colors,
         main = "Species Abundance Across Ethnic Groups\n(Including SCFA Producers)",
         fontsize = 10,
         annotation_names_row = TRUE)
dev.off()

# Print summary
cat("\nNumber of differentially abundant taxa:", length(significant_taxa))
cat("\nNumber of SCFA-producing taxa found:", length(intersect(selected_taxa, scfa_taxa)))
cat("\nTotal number of taxa in heatmap:", length(selected_taxa))
```

```{r}
# Combine significant taxa and SCFA producers
scfa_taxa = scfa_patterns
selected_taxa <- unique(c(significant_taxa, scfa_taxa))

# Extract and process abundance matrix
abundance_mat <- otu_table(phyloseq_filtered)
abundance_mat <- as(abundance_mat, "matrix")
abundance_mat <- abundance_mat[, selected_taxa]

# Convert to relative abundance
abundance_mat <- sweep(abundance_mat, 1, rowSums(abundance_mat), '/') * 100
sample_data <- data.frame(sample_data(phyloseq_filtered))
sample_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group <- factor(sample_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group, 
                                       levels = c("C", "I", "M"))

# Order samples by ethnicity
sample_order <- rownames(sample_data)[order(sample_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group)]
abundance_mat <- abundance_mat[sample_order, ]

# Transpose the matrix
abundance_mat <- t(abundance_mat)

# Calculate mean abundance for each taxa
mean_abundance <- rowMeans(abundance_mat)

# Create data frame with taxa info and mean abundance
taxa_info <- data.frame(
    Taxa = rownames(abundance_mat),
    Type = ifelse(rownames(abundance_mat) %in% scfa_taxa, "SCFA Producer", "Differential"),
    MeanAbundance = mean_abundance,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Order taxa by type and mean abundance
taxa_order <- taxa_info %>%
    arrange(desc(Type), desc(MeanAbundance)) %>%
    pull(Taxa)

# Reorder the abundance matrix
abundance_mat <- abundance_mat[taxa_order, ]

# Create annotation dataframe for columns (samples)
annotation_col <- data.frame(
    Ethnicity = sample_data$FREG5_Ethnic_Group[match(colnames(abundance_mat), rownames(sample_data))],
    row.names = colnames(abundance_mat)
)

# Create annotation for rows (taxa)
annotation_row <- data.frame(
    Type = ifelse(rownames(abundance_mat) %in% scfa_taxa, "SCFA Producer", "Differential"),
    row.names = rownames(abundance_mat)
)

# Create color schemes
annotation_colors <- list(
    Ethnicity = c("C" = "#9FDDCA", "I" = "#9EA3F1", "M" = "#E17887"),
    Type = c(`SCFA Producer` = "#FDB863", Differential = "#B2ABD2")
)
```


```{r}
# Create custom color palette for the heatmap
color_palette <- colorRampPalette(brewer.pal(11, "RdBu"))(100)



# Also save as PNG
png("figure/abundance_heatmap3.png", width = 2200, height = 1000, res = 150)
pheatmap(abundance_mat,
         scale = "row",
         color = color_palette,
         breaks = seq(-2, 2, length.out = 101),
         annotation_col = annotation_col,
         annotation_row = annotation_row,
         cluster_cols = FALSE,
         cluster_rows = FALSE,  # Disable clustering to maintain our manual ordering
         show_colnames = FALSE,
         annotation_colors = annotation_colors,
         main = "Species Abundance Across Ethnic Groups\n(Including SCFA Producers)",
         fontsize = 10,
         annotation_names_row = TRUE)
dev.off()
```


```{r}
# Print summary and ordering info
cat("\nNumber of differentially abundant taxa:", length(significant_taxa))
cat("\nNumber of SCFA-producing taxa found:", length(intersect(selected_taxa, scfa_taxa)))
cat("\nTotal number of taxa in heatmap:", length(selected_taxa))

# Print the taxa order for reference
cat("\n\nTaxa ordering (top to bottom):\n")
print(data.frame(
    Taxa = rownames(abundance_mat),
    Type = annotation_row$Type,
    MeanAbundance = mean_abundance[match(rownames(abundance_mat), names(mean_abundance))]
))
```

#enterotype
```{r}
library(vegan)
library(cluster)
# Filter samples to keep only C, I, M ethnic groups
phyloseq_filtered_CIM <- subset_samples(phyloseq_filtered, FREG5_Ethnic_Group %in% c("C", "I", "M"))

# Ensure samples are rows and genera are columns (transpose if needed)
otu_sample_matrix <- otu_table(phyloseq_filtered_CIM)

# Check the dimensions: should have 866 rows (samples) and 1336 columns (genera)
dim(otu_sample_matrix)


```
```{r}
# Calculate Bray-Curtis distance between samples
library(vegan)
dist_bc <- vegdist(otu_sample_matrix, method = "bray")

# Perform PAM clustering on samples
library(cluster)
pam_clustering <- pam(dist_bc, k = 3)  # Adjust k based on the number of enterotypes desired

# Assign enterotype labels to samples
sample_data(phyloseq_filtered_CIM)$Enterotype <- as.factor(pam_clustering$clustering)


# Create a table of enterotype proportions by ethnic group
enterotype_proportions <- prop.table(table(sample_data(phyloseq_filtered_CIM)$FREG5_Ethnic_Group,
                                           sample_data(phyloseq_filtered_CIM)$Enterotype), 1)

# Convert the table to a data frame for plotting
enterotype_proportions_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(enterotype_proportions))
colnames(enterotype_proportions_df) <- c("Ethnic_Group", "Enterotype", "Proportion")


# Plot enterotype proportions
ggplot(enterotype_proportions_df, aes(x = Ethnic_Group, y = Proportion, fill = Enterotype)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
  labs(title = "Enterotype Proportions by Ethnic Group",
       x = "Ethnic Group", y = "Proportion") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank())

```
#test
```{r}
# Create contingency table
contingency_table <- table(sample_data(phyloseq_filtered_CIM)$FREG5_Ethnic_Group,
                          sample_data(phyloseq_filtered_CIM)$Enterotype)

# Chi-square test
chi_result <- chisq.test(contingency_table)
print("Chi-square test results:")
print(chi_result)

# Fisher's exact test (more accurate for small sample sizes)
fisher_result <- fisher.test(contingency_table, simulate.p.value = TRUE)
print("\nFisher's exact test results:")
print(fisher_result)

# Post-hoc analysis - pairwise comparisons between ethnic groups for each enterotype
# Using proportion test for each enterotype
for(enterotype in unique(sample_data(phyloseq_filtered_CIM)$Enterotype)) {
  print(paste("\nAnalyzing Enterotype", enterotype))
  
  # Create subset for this enterotype
  subset_data <- contingency_table[, enterotype]
  total_counts <- rowSums(contingency_table)
  
  # Perform pairwise proportion tests
  ethnic_groups <- unique(sample_data(phyloseq_filtered_CIM)$FREG5_Ethnic_Group)
  for(i in 1:(length(ethnic_groups)-1)) {
    for(j in (i+1):length(ethnic_groups)) {
      group1 <- ethnic_groups[i]
      group2 <- ethnic_groups[j]
      
      test_result <- prop.test(
        x = c(subset_data[group1], subset_data[group2]),
        n = c(total_counts[group1], total_counts[group2])
      )
      
      print(paste("Comparison:", group1, "vs", group2))
      print(paste("p-value:", round(test_result$p.value, 4)))
    }
  }
}

# Optionally, adjust p-values for multiple comparisons
# You might want to collect all p-values and adjust them using p.adjust()
```

```{r}

```


```{r}
# Extract abundance data
otu_matrix <- as(otu_table(phyloseq_filtered_CIM), "matrix")
key_genera_abundance <- otu_matrix[, colnames(otu_matrix) %in% key_genera]

# Normalize to relative abundance if not already done
key_genera_abundance <- sweep(key_genera_abundance, 1, rowSums(key_genera_abundance), '/')

# Perform PAM clustering
pam_clustering <- pam(dist(key_genera_abundance), k = 3)

# Get cluster centers (medoids)
medoid_indices <- pam_clustering$medoids
cluster_centers <- key_genera_abundance[medoid_indices, ]

# Assign labels based on dominant genus in each medoid
enterotype_labels <- colnames(key_genera_abundance)[apply(cluster_centers, 1, which.max)]

# Assign enterotypes to samples
sample_data(phyloseq_filtered_CIM)$Enterotype <- factor(
    pam_clustering$clustering,
    levels = 1:3,
    labels = paste0("Enterotype_", enterotype_labels)
)

# Print cluster characteristics
print("Cluster medoid compositions:")
print(cluster_centers)
print("\nEnterotype labels:")
print(enterotype_labels)

# Create enterotype proportion table
enterotype_proportions <- prop.table(table(
    sample_data(phyloseq_filtered_CIM)$FREG5_Ethnic_Group,
    sample_data(phyloseq_filtered_CIM)$Enterotype
), 1)

# Convert to data frame for plotting
enterotype_proportions_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(enterotype_proportions))
colnames(enterotype_proportions_df) <- c("Ethnic_Group", "Enterotype", "Proportion")

# Plot
ggplot(enterotype_proportions_df, aes(x = Ethnic_Group, y = Proportion, fill = Enterotype)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "fill") +
    labs(title = "Enterotype Proportions by Ethnic Group",
         x = "Ethnic Group", 
         y = "Proportion") +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
    theme(legend.title = element_blank())

# Validate clustering
sil <- silhouette(pam_clustering)
print(paste("Average silhouette width:", mean(sil[,3])))

# Plot silhouette
plot(sil, main = "Silhouette Plot of Enterotype Clustering")

# PCA visualization
pca <- prcomp(key_genera_abundance, scale. = TRUE)
pca_df <- data.frame(
    PC1 = pca$x[,1],
    PC2 = pca$x[,2],
    Enterotype = sample_data(phyloseq_filtered_CIM)$Enterotype
)

ggplot(pca_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Enterotype)) +
    geom_point() +
    theme_minimal() +
    labs(title = "PCA Plot of Enterotypes")

# Additional analysis of cluster characteristics
print("\nCluster sizes:")
print(table(sample_data(phyloseq_filtered_CIM)$Enterotype))

# Calculate mean abundances for each genus in each cluster
cluster_means <- aggregate(key_genera_abundance, 
                         by = list(Enterotype = sample_data(phyloseq_filtered_CIM)$Enterotype), 
                         mean)
print("\nMean abundances in each cluster:")
print(cluster_means)
```

```{r}
# Calculate distance matrix
dist_matrix <- dist(key_genera_abundance)

# Perform PCoA
pcoa <- cmdscale(dist_matrix, eig = TRUE)

# Calculate variance explained
variance_explained <- round(100 * pcoa$eig/sum(pcoa$eig), 2)

# Create data frame for plotting
pcoa_df <- data.frame(
    PC1 = pcoa$points[,1],
    PC2 = pcoa$points[,2],
    Enterotype = sample_data(phyloseq_filtered_CIM)$Enterotype,
    Ethnic_Group = sample_data(phyloseq_filtered_CIM)$FREG5_Ethnic_Group
)

# Create PCoA plot with ellipses
ggplot(pcoa_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Enterotype)) +
    geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.6) +
    stat_ellipse(type = "t", level = 0.95) +
    labs(
        title = "PCoA Plot of Enterotypes",
        x = paste0("PCoA1 (", variance_explained[1], "%)"),
        y = paste0("PCoA2 (", variance_explained[2], "%)")
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
    theme(
        legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )

# Optional: Create a second plot with ellipses by ethnic group
ggplot(pcoa_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = Ethnic_Group)) +
    geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.6) +
    stat_ellipse(type = "t", level = 0.95) +
    labs(
        title = "PCoA Plot by Ethnic Group",
        x = paste0("PCoA1 (", variance_explained[1], "%)"),
        y = paste0("PCoA2 (", variance_explained[2], "%)"),
        color = "Ethnic Group"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set1") +
    theme(
        legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold")
    )

# If you want both enterotype and ethnic group information in one plot:
ggplot(pcoa_df, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2)) +
    geom_point(aes(color = Enterotype), size = 2, alpha = 0.6) +
    stat_ellipse(aes(color = Enterotype), type = "t", level = 0.95) +
    facet_wrap(~Ethnic_Group) +
    labs(
        title = "PCoA Plot of Enterotypes by Ethnic Group",
        x = paste0("PCoA1 (", variance_explained[1], "%)"),
        y = paste0("PCoA2 (", variance_explained[2], "%)")
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    scale_color_brewer(palette = "Set2") +
    theme(
        legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
```
